description: Mobile-first PWA where users press a button and get kidnapped to a random interesting place in their city via Uber/Lyft

tasks.title: Initialize Supabase project and install dependencies
tasks.description: Set up Supabase client library and environment configuration so the app can connect to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `pnpm add @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr` - exits with code 0
File `src/lib/supabase/client.ts` exists and exports a `createClient` function
File `src/lib/supabase/server.ts` exists and exports a server-side `createClient` function
File `.env.local.example` exists with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY placeholders
File `.gitignore` contains `.env.local`
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0 (or `npx tsc --noEmit`)

tasks.title: Create database schema migration for spots table
tasks.description: Define the spots table with all required fields for storing curated and community-submitted locations.
acceptanceCriteria: File `supabase/migrations/001_create_spots.sql` exists

SQL creates `spots` table with columns: id (uuid, PK), name (text, NOT NULL), description (text), category (text, NOT NULL), latitude (double precision, NOT NULL), longitude (double precision, NOT NULL), city (text, NOT NULL), hours (jsonb), tags (text[]), submitted_by (uuid, nullable), upvotes (integer, default 0), status (text, default 'pending', CHECK IN ('approved','pending','rejected')), created_at (timestamptz, default now())
SQL includes index on `city` and `status` columns
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Create database schema migration for profiles, trips, and votes tables
tasks.description: Define the user profiles, trips, and spot_votes tables to support trip logging, ratings, and upvoting.
acceptanceCriteria: File `supabase/migrations/002_create_profiles_trips_votes.sql` exists

SQL creates `profiles` table: id (uuid, PK, references auth.users), name (text), email (text), avatar_url (text), home_latitude (double precision), home_longitude (double precision), city (text), budget_max (integer, default 0 meaning no limit), is_admin (boolean, default false), created_at (timestamptz)

SQL creates `trips` table: id (uuid, PK), user_id (uuid, FK to profiles), spot_id (uuid, FK to spots), rating (integer, nullable, CHECK 1-5), notes (text, nullable), is_public (boolean, default true), created_at (timestamptz)
SQL creates `spot_votes` table: user_id (uuid, FK), spot_id (uuid, FK), created_at (timestamptz), PRIMARY KEY (user_id, spot_id)
SQL includes index on trips(user_id) and trips(spot_id)
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Create TypeScript types for database schema
tasks.description: Define TypeScript types matching the database schema so the app has type-safe data access.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/lib/types.ts` exists
Exports `Spot` type with all columns from spots table
Exports `Profile` type with all columns from profiles table
Exports `Trip` type with all columns from trips table
Exports `SpotVote` type with all columns from spot_votes table
Exports `SpotCategory` union type: 'food' | 'outdoors' | 'culture' | 'nightlife' | 'weird' | 'hidden-gem' | 'historic' | 'activity'
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build seed data generation script using Claude API
tasks.description: Create a Node script that uses the Claude API to generate 50 interesting Denver spots and outputs them as JSON for manual review before database import.
acceptanceCriteria: File `scripts/generate-spots.ts` exists
Script calls Claude API with a prompt requesting 50 interesting/unique Denver spots with fields: name, description, category, latitude, longitude, hours, tags
Script outputs results to `scripts/seed-spots.json`
File `scripts/import-spots.ts` exists and reads from `seed-spots.json`, inserts approved spots into Supabase
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Set up Supabase Auth with Google OAuth
tasks.description: Configure authentication so users can sign in with Google. Apple OAuth can be added later.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/auth/callback/route.ts` exists and handles OAuth callback
File `src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts` exists and refreshes auth session on each request
File `src/middleware.ts` exists and calls the Supabase middleware helper
File `src/components/auth/sign-in-button.tsx` exists and triggers Google OAuth sign-in via `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`
File `src/components/auth/sign-out-button.tsx` exists and calls `supabase.auth.signOut()`
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Create onboarding flow for home location setup
tasks.description: After first sign-in, prompt users to set their home location and city so the app can filter spots and estimate ride costs.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/onboarding/page.tsx` exists
Page requests browser geolocation permission via navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition
On geolocation success, reverse geocodes to city name using Nominatim API (https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse)
Displays detected city with option to manually change
Save button writes home_latitude, home_longitude, city to profiles table
After save, redirects to home page `/`
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/onboarding - page loads without console errors

tasks.title: Build the Kidnap Button home screen UI
tasks.description: Create the main home screen with a single dominant button. This is the core of the app — it should feel urgent, fun, and slightly dangerous. Dark theme.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/page.tsx` renders the home screen
Home screen has dark background (near-black)
Single large 'Kidnap Me' button centered on screen, taking up significant visual space
Button has hover/active states with visual feedback
If user is not authenticated, show sign-in button instead
If user has no profile/city set, redirect to /onboarding
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000 - page loads with dark background and prominent button visible

tasks.title: Build the spot selection API endpoint
tasks.description: Create a server-side API route that implements the kidnap algorithm: filters spots by city, time, budget, recency, and returns a weighted-random selection.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/api/kidnap/route.ts` exists with POST handler
Handler requires authenticated user (returns 401 if not)
Queries spots table filtered by: status = 'approved', city matches user's city
Filters out spots where current time falls outside spot hours (if hours defined)
Filters out spots user visited in last 30 days (queries trips table)
Filters out spots beyond user's budget using haversine distance * $1.50/mile heuristic
Selects from remaining spots using weighted random (weight = 1 + upvotes)
Returns selected spot as JSON with 200, or 404 with message if no spots match
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build destination reveal screen with animation
tasks.description: When the button is pressed, show a brief suspense moment then reveal the destination with spot details and ride booking options.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing 'Kidnap Me' button calls POST /api/kidnap and shows loading/suspense state
Suspense state lasts 1-2 seconds with animation (pulsing, spinning, or similar)
After suspense, destination card reveals with: spot name, category badge, short description
Reveal has entrance animation (slide up, fade in, or scale)
Two ride buttons shown below destination: 'Uber' and 'Lyft'
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000 - click Kidnap Me button - destination card appears

tasks.title: Implement Uber and Lyft deep link generation
tasks.description: Wire up the ride booking buttons to open Uber and Lyft apps with the destination pre-filled, and create a trip record on click.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/lib/ride-links.ts` exists
Exports `getUberDeepLink(lat, lng, name)` returning URL string using uber:// scheme with dropoff coordinates
Exports `getLyftDeepLink(lat, lng)` returning URL string using lyft:// scheme with destination coordinates
Exports `getUberWebFallback(lat, lng, name)` returning https://m.uber.com URL for when app not installed
Exports `getLyftWebFallback(lat, lng)` returning https://www.lyft.com URL for when app not installed
Clicking Uber/Lyft button creates a trip record via POST to `/api/trips` before opening the deep link
File `src/app/api/trips/route.ts` exists with POST handler that inserts trip (user_id, spot_id, created_at)
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build budget settings screen with slider
tasks.description: Create a settings page where users can set their maximum ride budget, which filters spot selection.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/settings/page.tsx` exists
Page shows a range slider from $5 to $50 with a 'No limit' option
Current budget value displayed above slider
Changing slider updates profile.budget_max in Supabase via API call
Settings page accessible from bottom navigation
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/settings - slider element visible and interactive

tasks.title: Build trip history page with rating and notes
tasks.description: Create the 'My Trips' page showing a chronological adventure log where users can rate trips and add notes.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/trips/page.tsx` exists
Page fetches user's trips from Supabase joined with spots table, ordered by created_at DESC
Each trip card shows: spot name, category badge, date (relative like '3 days ago'), star rating (if set)
Tapping a trip expands to show: spot description, notes field, rating selector
Star rating component allows setting 1-5 stars, saves via PATCH to `/api/trips/[id]`
Notes textarea saves on blur via PATCH to `/api/trips/[id]`
File `src/app/api/trips/[id]/route.ts` exists with PATCH handler for rating and notes
Trip count shown at top of page ('Kidnapped X times')
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/trips - page loads without console errors

tasks.title: Build spot submission form
tasks.description: Create a form where users can submit interesting places they know about for community review.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/submit/page.tsx` exists
Form fields: name (text input), description (textarea), category (dropdown with 8 categories), address (text input), hours (simplified time picker or text), tags (comma-separated text input)
Address field geocodes to lat/lng using Nominatim API on form submit
Submit creates a spot with status='pending' and submitted_by=current user ID via POST to `/api/spots`
File `src/app/api/spots/route.ts` exists with POST handler
Success state shows confirmation message ('Spot submitted for review!')
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/submit - form renders with all fields

tasks.title: Build admin moderation dashboard
tasks.description: Create a protected admin page to approve or reject community-submitted spots.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/admin/page.tsx` exists
Route is protected: redirects to / if user profile.is_admin is false
Page fetches spots where status='pending', ordered by created_at ASC
Each pending spot shows: name, description, category, submitted_by user name, map preview or lat/lng
Approve button sets spot status to 'approved' via PATCH `/api/spots/[id]`
Reject button sets spot status to 'rejected' via PATCH `/api/spots/[id]`
File `src/app/api/spots/[id]/route.ts` exists with PATCH handler (admin-only check)
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/admin - page loads (verify with admin user)

tasks.title: Implement spot upvoting with weighted selection
tasks.description: Add upvote/downvote toggle to spots and update the kidnap algorithm to weight selection by upvote count.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/components/upvote-button.tsx` exists
Component shows upvote count and filled/unfilled state based on whether current user has voted
Clicking toggles vote: POST `/api/spots/[id]/vote` inserts or deletes from spot_votes, updates spots.upvotes count
File `src/app/api/spots/[id]/vote/route.ts` exists with POST handler
Upvote button appears on trip detail (expanded trip card) and on feed items
Verify T-009 kidnap algorithm already uses upvotes for weighted random (weight = 1 + upvotes)
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build public feed page showing recent kidnaps
tasks.description: Create the social feed showing where other users in the same city got kidnapped recently.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/feed/page.tsx` exists
Page fetches recent public trips (is_public=true) in user's city, joined with spots and profiles, ordered by created_at DESC, limited to 50
Each feed item shows: user avatar + name, spot name, category badge, relative time ('2h ago'), star rating if set, notes if present
Feed items link to spot detail or expand inline
Empty state shows friendly message if no trips in the city yet
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/feed - page loads without console errors

tasks.title: Add trip privacy toggle (public/private)
tasks.description: Let users control whether their trips appear on the public feed.
acceptanceCriteria: Trip detail (expanded card on /trips) shows a toggle switch for public/private
Toggle updates trip.is_public via PATCH `/api/trips/[id]`
Default value for new trips is is_public=true
Private trips do not appear in /feed queries
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build bottom navigation bar
tasks.description: Create a persistent bottom nav for mobile-first navigation between the main sections of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/components/bottom-nav.tsx` exists
Nav bar fixed to bottom of viewport with 4 tabs: Home (button icon), Feed (people icon), Trips (list icon), Settings (gear icon)
Active tab visually highlighted
Nav uses Next.js Link for client-side navigation
Nav included in root layout, visible on all authenticated pages
Nav hidden on /onboarding and /admin pages
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000 - bottom nav visible with 4 tabs

tasks.title: Configure PWA manifest and service worker
tasks.description: Make the app installable as a PWA with proper manifest, icons, and offline shell support.
acceptanceCriteria: File `public/manifest.json` exists with: name='Kidnap', short_name='Kidnap', start_url='/', display='standalone', background_color (dark), theme_color, icons array with 192x192 and 512x512 sizes
File `src/app/layout.tsx` includes <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'>
File `src/app/layout.tsx` includes <meta name='theme-color'> and <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-capable'>
Service worker registered that caches app shell for offline loading (using next-pwa or manual SW)
Run `pnpm run build` - exits with code 0
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Build location detection utility with manual override
tasks.description: Create a reusable geolocation utility that detects user's city and allows manual override in settings.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/lib/geolocation.ts` exists
Exports `getCurrentPosition()` that wraps navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition in a Promise
Exports `reverseGeocode(lat, lng)` that calls Nominatim API and returns city name
Exports `calculateDistance(lat1, lng1, lat2, lng2)` using haversine formula, returns miles
Exports `estimateRideCost(distanceMiles)` returning distanceMiles * 1.5 (dollars)
Settings page (/settings) includes a city text input that updates profile.city in Supabase
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Add user profile page with trip stats
tasks.description: Create a profile page showing user info and adventure stats.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/app/profile/page.tsx` exists
Shows user avatar, name, city
Shows trip count ('Kidnapped X times')
Shows list of user's pending submitted spots (status='pending')
Sign out button at bottom of page
Accessible from settings page or nav
Run `pnpm run typecheck` - exits with code 0
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/profile - page loads without console errors

tasks.title: End-to-end smoke test of the full kidnap loop
tasks.description: Verify the complete user flow works: sign in → press button → see destination → tap ride link → trip appears in history.
acceptanceCriteria: agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000 - home page loads with dark theme
agent-browser: Kidnap Me button visible and clickable
agent-browser: click Kidnap Me - suspense animation appears, then destination card reveals
agent-browser: destination card shows spot name, category, description
agent-browser: Uber and Lyft buttons visible on destination card
agent-browser: open http://localhost:3000/trips - new trip appears in list
agent-browser: console shows no errors throughout flow
Run `pnpm run build` - exits with code 0
